evals.name: browser feature validation
evals.prompt: A user asks: verify the login feature in the browser, capture evidence, and check basic accessibility on the page. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response chooses a real browser runtime, detects/starts the dev server, prepares deterministic auth or fixtures, validates user-facing login behavior, checks accessibility basics, and reports artifacts.
assertions: The output chooses an available browser runtime such as built-in browser tools, the project browser runner, or Playwright.
The output says to detect or start the app/dev server before running browser actions.
The output validates user-facing login behavior rather than implementation details.
The output includes accessibility checks or assertions.
The output reports pass/fail/blocked plus useful artifacts such as screenshots, traces, logs, or snapshots when relevant.

evals.name: generate browser tests from manual flow
evals.prompt: A user describes a manual browser flow: open /checkout, add an item, apply a coupon, and verify the total. Describe how to turn this into browser automation or tests.
evals.expected_output: The response translates the flow into stable browser actions/assertions, controls data setup, avoids brittle sleeps, and verifies totals as rendered user-visible behavior.
assertions: The output translates each manual step into browser actions and assertions.
The output recommends stable locators such as role/text/test id rather than brittle CSS paths.
The output handles test data setup or deterministic fixture state.
The output avoids arbitrary sleeps in favor of browser assertions or waiting for conditions.
The output verifies the coupon and total as observable user-facing behavior.

evals.name: screenshot exploration not e2e only
evals.prompt: A user asks: open the settings page in a browser, inspect what is rendered, and take a screenshot. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses browser-automation for exploration and screenshot evidence, not only E2E testing; it selects built-in browser tools if available or Playwright fallback if not.
assertions: The output treats browser exploration and screenshots as in scope.
The output prefers built-in browser tools when available.
The output falls back to project browser tooling or Playwright when built-in tools are unavailable.
The output records screenshot or rendered-state evidence.
The output does not frame the task as only writing E2E tests.

evals.name: headless Pi screenshot evidence
evals.prompt: In Pi, no visible browser tool is exposed. A user wants visual verification of a rendered page. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses browser-automation with project tooling or playwright-skill fallback, prefers headless screenshots and manifests as evidence, waits for rendered state, and avoids insisting on headed mode.
assertions: The output does not require headed mode when no visible browser is exposed.
The output uses headless screenshots or manifests as visual evidence.
The output waits for a rendered selector, URL, accessibility snapshot, or other observable browser state.
The output reports artifact paths and pass/fail/blocked status.
The output uses playwright-skill only as a fallback/runtime rather than as the user-facing skill.
